creator: phantom@cris.crimea.ua
text:
hi,

On Tue, May 23, 2000 at 11:02:02PM +1000, Tony Maher wrote:
 
> >Number:         18775
> >Category:       docs
> >Synopsis:       spelling (Release Notes)
 
> In src/release/texts/i386/RELNOTES.TXT the following passage has
> some interesting wording.
> 
> "Floating point exceptions for new processes (devide-by-zero,
>                                               ^^^^^^
> under/overflow, invalid range etc.) are now disabled by default. Use
> fpsetmask(3) to reenable those you need. Note that integer
> device-by-zero is not covered by the FPU and will still trap after"
> ^^^^^^

Shouldn't it be "divide" ? I mean both cases.

--
